examples.title: File Open, Read, Write Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates basic file operations using file descriptors
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the typical sequence: open with flags, write initial data, seek to reposition, read data, seek to end for append, then close. Each operation affects the file position pointer stored in the per-process open file table entry.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for read/write where n is bytes transferred, O(1) for open/close/seek
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for buffer used in read/write operations, O(1) for file descriptors

examples.title: File Locking Example
examples.description: Shows how to implement file locking for coordinated access
examples.explanation: flock() provides whole-file locking. LOCK_SH (shared) allows multiple readers simultaneously. LOCK_EX (exclusive) blocks all other locks. LOCK_UN releases the lock. This prevents race conditions when multiple processes access the same file.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for lock operations, O(n) for read/write
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for lock structures, O(n) for I/O buffers

examples.title: Two-Process File Sharing with Fork
examples.description: Demonstrates file descriptor inheritance after fork()
examples.explanation: After fork(), child inherits parent's file descriptors pointing to same system-wide file table entry, so they share the same file position. This demonstrates that file position is NOT stored in the per-process table alone - it's in the system table. Closing in child doesn't affect parent (different descriptor table entries), but both see changes to the shared file offset.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for buffer, O(1) for descriptors
